salientSummary: Background 5-min auto-fetch on the focused repo via AutoFetchController @Observable timer; retargets on focus change and Settings toggle change; hard-stops on window close; 6 ViewModel tests (bare-upstream fixtures, 1s interval override) + 2 authored XCUITests (skipped pending interval-override launch-arg); 201/201 KiteTests green under the M8 skip list; VAL-NET-006 and VAL-NET-007 fulfilled.
whatWasImplemented: Added Sources/ViewModels/AutoFetchController.swift — @Observable @MainActor final class with retarget(to:), stop(), isRunning, and a tunable intervalSeconds (default 300). The timer loop sleeps first then fetches, re-reads persistence.settings.autoFetchEnabled on each tick so a toggle-off during the wait window silently skips the fetch, and guards against stale focus via currentFocusID so a retarget that swaps focus mid-sleep stops rather than fetching on the prior repo. Wired Sources/App/KiteApp.swift to create AutoFetchController(ops:persistence:) after NetworkOps + PersistenceStore and inject via .environment on both WindowGroup and Settings scenes. Extended Sources/Views/RootView.swift with @Environment(RepoStore.self) + @Environment(PersistenceStore.self) + @Environment(AutoFetchController.self) bindings, a .task(id: repoStore.focus?.repo.id) that calls autoFetch.retarget(to: repoStore.focus), .onChange(of: persistence.settings.autoFetchEnabled) calling retarget to re-evaluate the toggle, and .onDisappear calling autoFetch.stop(). Settings > General already binds persistence.setAutoFetchEnabled(_:); verified it routes correctly by running the full KiteTests suite. Added Tests/KiteTests/ViewModelTests/AutoFetchControllerTests.swift (6 tests using bare + author + tracker fixture pattern, 1s interval override, UserDefaults(suiteName:) per test, indirect observation via git rev-parse origin/main). Added Tests/KiteUITests/AutoFetchUITests.swift (2 authored XCUITests gated with XCTSkipIf, rationale: production 5-min interval is too long for a CI XCUITest window without an interval-override launch-arg hook; timer behavior is instead covered by the ViewModelTests suite).
whatWasLeftUndone: XCUITest suite is authored but intentionally skipped — both tests XCTSkipIf with documented rationale. Timer correctness is covered by the ViewModel tests with a 1s override. A future feature could add a -KITE_FIXTURE_AUTOFETCH_INTERVAL launch arg (gated on isRunningUnderXCTest) to un-skip the XCUI suite; not in scope for M5-auto-fetch.

commandsRun.observation: 201 tests in 30 suites passed after ~97s (includes the 6 new AutoFetchControllerTests).

interactiveChecks.action: UI verification deferred: XCUITest harness blocked by host TCC; authored suite Tests/KiteUITests/AutoFetchUITests.swift is committed with XCTSkipIf gates and documented rationale. Timer behavior is covered by KiteTests/AutoFetchControllerTests against real bare-upstream fixtures.
interactiveChecks.observed: Not run interactively in this environment — called out explicitly per swiftui-worker SKILL step 8.

discoveredIssues.description: AutoFetchUITests is committed with both cases XCTSkipIf'd because the production AutoFetchController interval is 300s and there's no interval-override launch-arg hook yet. A later feature (candidate: M8-commands-and-menu or a dedicated test-harness feature) could add `-KITE_FIXTURE_AUTOFETCH_INTERVAL <seconds>` gated on isRunningUnderXCTest to un-skip these tests.
